context.description: A nonfiction book passage explains bodily self-regulation and homeostasis, using hunger, thirst, breathing, sleep, and waste elimination as examples. It argues that much of daily behavior is driven by the body's need to maintain balance rather than by free choice. The visible text ends mid-thought while discussing the limits of changing these systems voluntarily.
normalization_notes: Source language detected as German; normalized surface values translated to English where appropriate.
Page text is philosophical/explanatory prose from a nonfiction book; content ends mid-sentence.

rows.paragraph_text: Body systems usually work in the background to maintain balance, becoming noticeable only when something goes wrong. Daily behavior is framed as an effort to preserve this balance, with hunger, thirst, waste elimination, sleep, and other needs presented as bodily pressures that limit free choice.

content.free_text: book_page book_content chapter_section Nonfiction book page on homeostasis, bodily regulation, survival-oriented behavior, and the limits of free will.
